- Adjusted PRD status to "Draft — Pending Approval" and reframed §2.2 to honestly position early work as exploratory, not final. PRD is now ready to send for review.

- Fixed the `@react-pdf/renderer` build type error in `app/app/api/leads/route.ts` by passing a typed document element directly to `renderToBuffer`.

- Started repo publish step for this prompt: preparing the current ACC rollback/build-verified changes for commit and push to the existing remote branch.

- Committed the staged ACC rollback + docs reorganization work on branch `cesar` and pushed it to `origin/cesar` successfully.
- Reviewed `codespace-convo.md` to recover the earlier accent values (`#DC673B`, `#F9D251`, `#1B1B1B`) and applied the near-black token back into the live ACC theme as the shared deep/dark color.
- Synced the near-black update through the runtime theme (`app/app/globals.css`), PDF/email output (`app/lib/pdf/playbook.tsx`, `app/app/api/leads/route.ts`), and source-of-truth docs (`docs/design-system.md`, `docs/PRD.md`, `docs/DESIGN.md`, `README.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`).
- Validated the near-black update: no errors in the edited runtime files, and old dark hexes now remain only inside `codespace-convo.md` as historical conversation notes rather than active project source.
- Started a project-wide frontend accent rollout to replace the old gold/terracotta/blush emphasis system with the recovered orange (`#DC673B`) + yellow (`#F9D251`) pairing, while keeping sage as the primary brand color and `#1B1B1B` as the deep anchor.
- Completed the frontend accent rollout across shared UI (`globals.css`, `Badge`, `Button`, `Input`, `PublicNav`, `VideoPlayer`), public marketing pages, and dashboard/admin event surfaces so orange now handles secondary emphasis and yellow stays a smaller highlight accent.
- Synced the new accent model into the design docs and repo instructions (`docs/design-system.md`, `docs/PRD.md`, `docs/DESIGN.md`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`) so future frontend work follows the same token strategy.
- Rebuilt the app successfully after the final homepage hero tweak and verified the production render on `http://localhost:3004`, confirming root tokens resolve to `#1B1B1B`, `#DC673B`, and `#F9D251`, and the homepage hero emphasis is actually rendering the orange accent in the browser.
- Started the follow-up UI pass for broader page checks and mobile navigation behavior, targeting the public navbar plus dashboard/admin sidebars so all three open as mobile side panels instead of full-screen overlays or permanently visible sidebars.
- Added a shared mobile drawer pattern in `app/components/layout/MobileSidePanel.tsx` and refactored `PublicNav`, `DashboardNav`, and `AdminNav` to open side panels on mobile while preserving the existing desktop navigation layouts.
- Updated `app/app/(dashboard)/layout.tsx` and `app/app/(admin)/layout.tsx` so mobile content clears the new fixed top bars with cleaner small-screen padding.

- Verified the mobile navigation behavior in browser on public, member, and admin pages: each menu opens as a true side panel (narrower than the viewport), links navigate correctly, and the recent layout/nav changes still pass `npm run build`.
- Started a full responsive-design pass across the ACC frontend, focusing on mobile-first layout fixes for public marketing pages, auth flows, clinician search, and dashboard/admin screens beyond the navigation shell.
- Implemented the core responsive UI pass across the most visible ACC surfaces: public marketing pages, `join`, auth screens, clinician directory filters, shared cards/footer, dashboard billing, and the admin members/resources/events tables.
- Converted the heaviest mobile problem areas from cramped multi-column/table layouts into stacked card or single-column patterns while preserving the richer desktop versions for larger screens.
- Re-ran `npm run build` from `app/` after the responsive changes and confirmed the production build still passes with no build-time errors.
- Verified the responsive pass in browser at a strict mobile viewport (`390px` wide) on key routes including `/`, `/find-a-clinician`, `/join`, `/admin/members`, and `/dashboard/billing`; the new mobile layouts, card stacks, and filter drawer rendered correctly.

## 2026-04-25
- Started repo publish step for the latest prompt: preparing the verified navbar/footer/member-public UI polish changes for commit and push to `origin/cesar` while keeping local helper files out of the commit.
- Started a new UI expansion pass: making the public navbar CTA green, reorganizing the shared footer, and upgrading any thinner public/dashboard routes that still need richer responsive UI treatment in the current design system.
- Started the repo-instruction follow-up from the latest prompt: adding an explicit rule that ACC work should run `npm run build` when needed and should investigate/fix relevant console error logs before completion, then preparing the repo for another push to `origin/cesar`.
- Started the admin-shell refresh requested in the latest prompt: shifting the admin sidebar/drawer to orange, changing the admin footer action to “Log out,” and tightening the role split so sage leads text emphasis while orange becomes a structural/admin accent.
- Started a follow-up UI polish pass to lock the desktop dashboard/admin shell so the sidebar stays fully visible while only the main content panel scrolls.
- Started a color-language cleanup pass to stop using orange for text emphasis, test yellow as the warmer complement, and bring hero text emphasis back closer to the sage family where it reads better.
- Completed the desktop shell adjustment for both dashboard and admin layouts: on desktop the document no longer scrolls, the sidebar stays fully visible, and only the main content panel scrolls.
- Tested a cleaner accent split by removing orange text emphasis from the visible frontend and shifting text emphasis toward sage + yellow instead, while leaving orange mainly for fills, borders, and selective surface accents.
- Verified the new color direction in-browser on the homepage: the hero emphasis now renders as sage-tinted text, the secondary hero/problem emphasis renders as yellow, and the public top-bar CTA text also renders yellow instead of orange.
- Re-validated the shell behavior in-browser on both `/dashboard/events` and `/admin/events`: the page document itself is not scrollable on desktop, the main panel scrolls independently, and each desktop sidebar remains fully visible with hidden sidebar overflow.
- Re-ran `npm run build` from `app/` after the shell + accent cleanup and confirmed the production build still passes cleanly.
- Completed the requested admin-shell refresh: the admin sidebar and mobile drawer now use the orange accent system, and the old “Back to site” action was replaced with “Log out” linking to `/sign-in`.
- Updated the homepage hero so the serif emphasis “Find your community.” now renders in green/sage via a semantic role token instead of falling back to white.
- Rewrote `docs/design-system.md` and `docs/DESIGN.md`, then synced `docs/PRD.md` and `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, to clearly codify the new hierarchy: sage-first brand, orange admin shell / warm surfaces, yellow micro-highlights.
- Found and fixed a runtime token issue while validating: semantic alias variables such as `--color-admin-sidebar` and `--color-hero-tagline` must live in plain `:root` CSS instead of only inside Tailwind `@theme inline`, otherwise the browser resolves them as empty values.
- Final validation complete: `npm run build` passes after the token fix, homepage hero emphasis computes to `rgb(107, 133, 114)`, and the admin sidebar computes to `rgb(220, 103, 59)` with the visible footer action reading `Log out`.
- Updated `.github/copilot-instructions.md` again to explicitly require `npm run build` for substantial / production-impacting work and to treat relevant browser console errors as mandatory verification items to reproduce, fix, and re-check.
- Re-ran `npm run build` from `app/` for this instruction-update prompt and confirmed the production build still passes.
- Pushed the latest repo state to `origin/cesar` with commit `7ad39516626ca22014cf49029d2c7f2dea3dd8fe` (`chore: refresh UI and verification instructions`), then prepared a final follow-up log-only sync so `.github/progress.md` stays current at task close.
- Completed the latest public/member UI polish pass: the public navbar CTA now stays sage green, the shared footer is reorganized into a stronger CTA + structured link columns, and the thinner member/public pages gained fuller responsive layouts.
- Expanded `dashboard/resources` into a richer library experience with stats, featured content, curated collections, clearer empty states, and filter controls that work across both sidebar and drawer layouts.
- Expanded `dashboard/network` into a fuller referral workspace with stats, network activity, clinician fit data, sorting/filtering, and stronger member cards for referral decisions.
- Upgraded `/coming-soon` with a more structured header/footer system that better matches the ACC brand language while preserving the lead-capture flow.
- During browser validation, found and fixed a shared responsive bug: `MobileSidePanel` was hidden at `md` while several filter triggers stayed visible until `lg`, so drawers silently failed on tablet widths. Added a shared `hideAbove` breakpoint prop and applied it to the filter-drawer pages (`/find-a-clinician`, `/dashboard/resources`, `/dashboard/network`).
- Final validation for this prompt is complete: file-level checks are clean, `npm run build` passes from `app/` with no warnings, the public navbar CTA computes to `rgb(74, 93, 78)`, and the updated drawers/pages were browser-verified on `/`, `/coming-soon`, `/find-a-clinician`, `/dashboard/resources`, and `/dashboard/network`.
- Repo publish complete for the verified UI polish pass: pushed branch `cesar` to `origin` at commit `187887e` (`feat: polish public and member UI`) while keeping local helper artifacts (`app/.vscode/`, `codespace-convo.md`) out of the commit.
